hypothesis: Incorporating a confidence estimation signal into E2EFOLD's Gumbel-Softmax sampling temperature—dynamically increasing τ for residue positions where the encoder's predicted amino acid distribution is highly uncertain (high entropy) and decreasing τ where it is confident (low entropy)—will improve both sequence design self-consistency and structure prediction accuracy compared to a globally annealed temperature schedule, because position-specific uncertainty-aware discretization will reduce error propagation from ambiguous residues through the decoder while preserving the structural constraints encoded by high-confidence positions.
